expected_effect: Hold most of screen_0004's raw edge and arb-discipline gain while reducing fee spend and restoring retail-edge competitiveness.
expected_failure_signature: If fee spend stays elevated and retail edge does not recover, the line is overspending; if raw edge collapses quickly, the overlay was carrying the result and the split is not robust.
expected_win_condition: Stay near screen_0004 on mean_edge while bringing time-weighted mean fee and retail edge back toward the incumbent.
hidden_coupling_removed: The harder toxic overlay now buys raw edge, but retail recovery is still tied too directly to the same fee spend, so reclaim must happen through a narrower non-toxic path.
rationale: Exploit screen_0004 as the strongest raw split variant by keeping its stronger toxic overlay but reclaiming some retail edge and mean-fee spend through a narrower non-toxic recovery path instead of broad cheapening.
title: Baseline split with reclaimed retail spend
why_not_coefficient_retune: This keeps the two-channel split but changes how retail recovery is reintroduced after the harder toxic overlay, instead of globally relaxing the whole surface.
